sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash in Round 34 of the Serie A sees struggling Venezia, positioned 18th with only 26 points, host Milan, who sit in 9th place with a more comfortable 57 points. This matchup at Stadio Pierluigi Penzo in Venice promises an intriguing battle between a team fighting relegation and another looking to solidify their mid-table standing. The referee for the game, Gianluca Manganiello, averages 4.5 yellow cards and 0.31 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially feisty encounter.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Historically, Milan have dominated Venezia in their last 5 encounters, securing 4 wins with 1 draw and no losses for Venezia. Their most recent meeting on September 14, 2024, ended in a convincing 4-0 victory for Milan, with Christian Pulisic earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.5 Sofascore rating. Milan have also maintained a clean sheet in their last 3 head-to-head games, while Venezia have failed to keep a clean sheet in all 5 of these matches, indicating a significant defensive challenge for the home side.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Venezia are coming off a streak of no losses in their last 3 general matches, though they’ve seen less than 2.5 goals in 8 of their last 10 games, pointing to a low-scoring trend. On the other hand, Milan have a potent attack, with more than 2.5 goals in 8 of their last 10 matches, and they’ve consistently pushed for over 10.5 corners in their last 5 games, contrasting with Venezia’s trend of under 10.5 corners in 5 of their last 7. Both teams have seen less than 4.5 cards in recent head-to-heads (8 of 9 matches), suggesting discipline despite the competitive nature of this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Venezia’s Key Players and Struggles
sections.text: Venezia will rely on players like Gaetano Oristanio, a forward with a league rank of 5 for successful dribbles per game at 1.6, to create attacking opportunities. Hans Nicolussi Caviglia has also been a standout with 4 goals and 2 assists, overperforming his expected goals of 2.21 by 1.7888. However, their season stats are worrying, with only 28 goals scored (17th in the league) and 49 conceded (13th for least goals conceded), alongside a poor conversion rate of big chances at 26.23% (19th in the league). Defensively, players like Marin Šverko and Jay Idzes will need to step up against Milan’s firepower.

sections.subtitle: Milan’s Star Power and Strengths
sections.text: Milan boast several high-ranking players, including Christian Pulisic, who leads the league with 63 key passes (1st) and has 8 assists (2nd), alongside 10 goals. Rafael Leão is another threat, ranking 3rd for successful dribbles per game at 1.8, 4th for key passes with 58, and also tied for 2nd with 8 assists. Theo Hernández, a standout in past matchups against Venezia with an average Sofascore rating of 8.2, 4 goals, and 1 assist over 4 starts, will be crucial defensively and offensively. Fikayo Tomori adds solidity at the back, ranking 4th with a 69.5% ground duels won rate.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistics
sections.text: Venezia struggle with possession, averaging 44.83% (16th in the league), and their accurate passes percentage of 78.34% ranks 18th, hinting at difficulties in building play. Milan, on the other hand, are likely to exploit Venezia’s defensive frailties, given the home side’s 79 opponent big chances created (15th in the league) and a high opponent shots per game rate of 14.74 (1st). Venezia’s strength lies in late-game scoring, with 28.57% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes, while Milan peak between the 61st and 75th minutes with 17.95% of their goals.

sections.subtitle: In-Form Players to Watch
sections.text: For Venezia, goalkeeper Filip Stanković has been in form over the last 5 matches with an average Sofascore rating of 7.5, potentially key to keeping Milan at bay. Milan’s Mike Maignan mirrors this form in goal, boasting a 7.62 average rating over the same period, suggesting a tight battle between the posts. These performances could be decisive in a match where defensive resilience will be tested.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Venezia will miss defender Michael Svoboda, who has contributed 1 goal and holds a solid 6.86 average rating, potentially weakening their backline. Milan, meanwhile, are without defender Matteo Gabbia, who also has a 7.0 rating and 1 goal, as well as Emerson Royal, both of whom could impact their defensive depth. These absences might force tactical adjustments on both sides.
